name: Arduino
description: Arduino is an open-source electronics platform comprising a family of microcontroller boards (Arduino Uno, Mega, Nano, etc.) and an integrated development environment (IDE). Arduino boards provide digital and analogue I/O pins for connecting sensors and actuators, and the Arduino language (C/C++ with simplified libraries) makes embedded programming accessible to beginners. The Arduino ecosystem includes thousands of libraries for sensors, displays, communication modules, and motor drivers.
whyUseIt: Arduino is the most accessible entry point to embedded systems and robotics hardware. Its low cost (boards from $5-30 AUD), extensive documentation, massive community, and plug-and-play sensor/actuator libraries let you go from idea to working prototype in hours. It is the go-to platform for learning robotics hardware, building line followers, controlling servos, reading sensors, and prototyping ideas before moving to more powerful platforms like STM32 or ESP32.
